reasoning: Year-end funding pressures typically cause a localized peak in primary credit borrowing around December 31, which saw a level of 9664. As year-end operations unwind in early January, borrowing historically drops significantly (e.g., in early 2024 and 2025). Thus, the Jan 11 resolution (Jan 7 data) is highly unlikely to exceed the Dec 31 peak. By Feb 3 (Jan 28 month-end data), the underlying secular uptrend (driven partly by ongoing QT and reserve effects) will bring the base level higher, making a month-end spike more competitive with the year-end value. By April 4 (April 1 Q1-end data), the sustained ~600/month positive trend seen since mid-2025 is expected to raise the baseline enough that, combined with quarter-end pressures, the borrowing amount will likely eclipse the 9664 mark. Probabilities are therefore progressively higher, reverting toward maximum uncertainty.
